name: Papyrus
description: Writing material harvested in the Nile valley and traded from Egypt.
note: Egyptian monopoly represented by the Alexandria production-port anchor

name: Silphium
description: A rare Cyrenaican aromatic and medicinal plant.
note: Barca is a Cyrenaica proxy and the later extinction is reserved for M10

name: Naphtha and Bitumen
description: Natural petroleum and bitumen used as sealant fuel and adhesive.
note: Hit is the source-labelled Mesopotamian bitumen anchor

name: Jade
description: Nephrite prized for carving adornment and exchange along the Tarim routes.
note: Khotan is the direct installed jade anchor

name: Camels
description: Pack camels supporting desert and long-distance overland exchange.
note: Medina is a deliberately narrow Arabian dromedary-trade proxy
